title: AUTOMATED SYSTEMS FOR PREPARING SEED TISSUE SAMPLES, AND ASSOCIATED METHODS
abstract: A seed sampling system is provided comprising an automated seed loading assembly operable to singulate seeds from a plurality of seeds or enable loading of individually stored seeds and an automated seed sampling assembly comprising at least one sampling module operable to remove tissue samples from one of the singulated seeds. The system also includes an automated seed transport assembly comprising at least one retention member operable to transfer the singulated seeds from at least one elevator unit of the seed loading assembly to the at least one sampling module of the seed sampling assembly. In connection therewith, the at least one sampling module includes multiple sampling locations, each associated with a sampler, where the at least one sampling module is operable to remove tissue samples from seeds at one of sampling locations while another one of the sampling locations is cleaned to remove residual seed tissue therefrom.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An automated seed sampling assembly comprising at least one sampling module, the at least one sampling module including multiple sampling locations each associated with a sampler, wherein the at least one sampling module is operable to remove tissue samples from seeds at one of sampling locations while another one of the sampling locations is cleaned to remove residual seed tissue therefrom. 2. The assembly of claim 1, wherein the at least one sampling module includes a first sampling module and a second sampling module, and wherein the first sampling module is removable from the seed sampling assembly as a unit and independent of the second sampling module. 3. The assembly of claim 1, wherein the at least one sampling module further includes at least one blow off jet and at least one collection port at each of the sampling locations for use in cleaning the sampling locations to remove residual seed tissue therefrom. 4. The assembly of claim 3, further comprising: at least one nozzle block coupled to the at least one sampling module, the at least one nozzle block configured to receive, from the at least one sampling module, the tissue samples removed from the seeds and deposit the tissue samples in one or more wells of a sample collection plate; and at least one purge block; wherein the at least one nozzle block is further configured receive the removed residual seed tissue from the at least one collection port of the at least one sampling module and to engage the at least one purge block to transport the removed residual seed tissue thereto for disposal. 5. The assembly of claim 1, wherein the at least one sampling module further includes a moveable seed grip assembly configured to move a first seed toward the sampler 

 associated with one of the sampling locations to thereby facilitate removal of a tissue sample from the first seed while another one of the sampling locations is cleaned, and subsequently to move a second seed toward the sampler associated with the another one of the sampling locations to thereby facilitate removal of a tissue sample from the second seed while the one of the sampling locations is cleaned. 6. The assembly of claim 5, wherein the seed grip assembly includes a first pair of arms configured to hold the first seed therebetween, and a second pair of arms configured to hold the second seed therebetween. 7. The assembly of claim 6, wherein the at least one sampling module further includes a sample collection funnel disposed adjacent each of the samplers for collecting tissue samples removed from seeds by each of the samplers. 8. The assembly of claim 6, wherein the at least one sampling module further includes at least one sensor disposed adjacent each of the samplers, the at least one sensor configured to determine a position of a seed held by the seed grip assembly. 9. The assembly of claim 7, wherein the at least one sampling module further includes at least one sensor positioned on the seed grip assembly in association with at least one of the multiple sampling locations, the at least one sensor configured to: determine a location of a seed prior the seed being received by the seed grip assembly at the at least one of the multiple sampling locations; and determine a position of the sampler at the at least one sampling location. 10. The assembly of claim 1, wherein the at least one sampling module includes multiple sampling modules. 11. The assembly of claim 10, wherein the multiple sampling modules include at least six sampling modules. 12. A seed sampling system comprising: an automated seed loading assembly operable to singulate seeds from a plurality of seeds, the seed loading assembly comprising multiple laterally spaced elevator units, each of the elevator units operable to actuate one of the singulated seeds into a position generally above the elevator unit; an automated seed sampling assembly comprising multiple laterally spaced sampling modules, each of the sampling modules operable to remove tissue samples from one of the singulated seeds; and an automated seed transport assembly comprising multiple laterally spaced retention members operable to transfer the singulated seeds from the elevator units of the seed loading assembly to the sampling modules of the seed sampling assembly; wherein the lateral spacing between the elevator units of the seed loading assembly, the lateral spacing between the sampling modules of the automated seed sampling assembly, and the lateral spacing between the retention members of the automated seed transport assembly are the same. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein the retention members are configured to orient the seeds received from the elevator units prior to presenting the seeds to the sampling modules. 14. The system of claim 13, wherein each of the retention members includes a suction cup configured to hold the singulated seeds received from the elevator units. 15. The system of claim 12, wherein the automated seed sampling assembly includes a number of sampling modules corresponding to a number of the retention members of the seed transport assembly. 16. The system of claim 12, further comprising: a sample collection assembly configured to receive, from the seed sampling assembly, the tissue samples removed from the seeds; and an imaging assembly adjacent the sample collection assembly configured to collect image data of the tissue samples when received at the sample collection assembly, and/or at least one 

 sensor adjacent the sample collection assembly configured to measure a mass of tissue samples received at the sample collection assembly. 17. The system of claim 12, further comprising a seed imaging assembly configured to capture image data of the singulated seeds, while the singulated seeds are at the seed transport assembly. 18. The system of claim 12, further comprising a computing device configured to adjust operation of at least one component of the assembly, relating to the singulated seeds, between when the seed loading assembly singulates the seeds and when the seed sampling assembly removes tissue from the singulated seeds. 19. An automated method for removing tissue samples from seeds, the method comprising: singulating a seed from a plurality of seeds; engaging the singulated seed with a retention member of an automated seed transport assembly; orienting the seed at the retention member, and moving the oriented seed to a sampling module of an automated seed sampling assembly; and removing a tissue sample from the singulated seed at the sampling module. 20. The method of claim 19, further comprising collecting, by an imaging assembly, image data of the seed; and wherein orienting the seed includes orienting the seed to a desired orientation based on the collected image data of the seed. 21. The method of claim 19 further comprising receiving the tissue sample removed from the singulated seed in a sample plate and receiving the singulated seed from which the tissue sample is removed in a seed tray. 22. The method of claim 21, further comprising assigning an identification to the seed and to the tissue sample removed from the seed, whereby the identification can be used to subsequently identify the seed in the seed tray and the corresponding tissue sample in the sample plate. 23. The method of claim 19 further comprising analyzing the tissue sample for one or more characteristics selected from the group consisting of a genetic marker, a single nucleotide polymorphism, a simple sequence repeat, a restriction fragment length polymorphism, a haplotype, a tag S P, an alleles of a genetic marker, a gene, a DNA-derived sequence, an RNA- derived sequence, a promoter, a 5' untranslated region of a gene, a 3' untranslated region of a gene, microRNA, siRNA, a QTL, a satellite marker, a transgene, mRNA, ds mRNA, a transcriptional profile, and a methylation pattern. 24. The method of claim 19 further comprising analyzing the tissue sample and either selecting or not selecting the seed from which the tissue sample is removed based on the presence of one or more characteristics in the tissue sample that are genetically linked with a QTL selected from the group consisting of herbicide tolerance, disease resistance, insect or pest resistance, altered fatty acid, protein or carbohydrate metabolism, increased grain yield, increased oil, increased nutritional content, increased growth rates, enhanced stress tolerance, preferred maturity, enhanced organoleptic properties, altered morphological characteristics, other agronomic traits, traits for industrial uses, traits for improved consumer appeal, and a combination of traits as a multiple trait index. 25. The method of claim 19 further comprising analyzing the tissue sample and either selecting or not selecting the seed from which the tissue sample is removed based on the presence of one or more characteristics in the tissue sample that are genetically linked with a haplotype associated with a QTL selected from the group consisting of herbicide tolerance, disease resistance, insect or pest resistance, altered fatty acid, protein or carbohydrate metabolism, increased grain yield, increased oil, increased nutritional content, increased growth rates, enhanced stress tolerance, preferred maturity, enhanced organoleptic properties, altered 

 morphological characteristics, other agronomic traits, traits for industrial uses, traits for improved consumer appeal, and a combination of traits as a multiple trait index. 26. The method of claim 19, wherein removing the tissue sample from the singulated seed at the sampling module includes removing the tissue sample from the singulated seed at a first sampling location of the sampling module; further comprising removing residual seed tissue from a second sampling location of the sampling module, via a collection port, at about the same time the tissue is removed from the singulated seed at the first sampling location of the sampling module. 27. The method of claim 19, wherein singulating the seed from the plurality of seeds includes singulating a pre-identified and individually contained, located, or managed seed from the plurality of seeds.
